data.title: Intuitive Machines Stock Plunges Another 26% After Troublesome Lunar Landing — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Space is hard. That can make space-related stocks volatile.

Shares of Intuitive Machines dropped 22% to $8.77 on Friday, after falling 20% on Thursday.

The S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.6% and 0.5%, respectively.

Intuitive Machines shares started falling Thursday after difficulties landing the company's Athena spaceship near the moon's south pole. The craft touched down around noon Eastern time, but NASA's broadcast of the landing finished without clear information about the status of the craft — whether it was upright and if all systems were operating as intended.

Intuitive CEO Steve Altemus said at a NASA press conference Thursday afternoon that he didn't believe Athena was upright.

"I think we can all agree...that landing on the moon is extremely hard," said Nicola Fox, NASA's associate director at the Science Missions Directorate. "Intuitive Machines was aiming to land in a place that humanity has not been before."

She added that the lander was returning data back from the moon. Intuitive, however, called an end to the mission on Friday, so it looks like as if all the scientific payloads won't operate as intended.

Athena was the company's second landing. Its Odysseus lander touched down on the moon in February 2024. Shares were below $3 apiece early in 2024 and traded above $10 around the time of the landing. Shares ended the year at above $18 a share.

NASA called the Odysseus mission a success. It was the first time the U.S. had landed on the moon in 50-plus years. Odysseus, however, tipped over on landing.

Both landings were part of NASA's Commercial Lunar Payload Services program, which aims to have NASA contract for a payload delivery and then let commercial companies take care of delivery. In the past, NASA would partner with commercial companies for parts and equipment, but NASA would design and launch the ships.

"I'll use aviation as an example...we don't build airplanes, we don't run airlines, but we are a customer and user of those," said NASA associate administrator Clayton Turner. "That's how you can imagine space going forward."

"Intuitive Machines will try again," wrote Benchmark analyst Josh Sullivan in a report Friday. "We view the move in Intuitive Machines stock as an overreaction given [company] backlog, and the context that space can often be an iteration process."

Sullivan rates shares Buy and has a $16 price target on the stock. Overall, seven of nine, or almost 80% of analysts covering shares, rate them Buy. The average analyst Buy-rating ratio for stocks in the S&P 500 is about 55%. The average analyst price target for Intuitive Machines' stock is about $18.

Despite the positive Wall Street sentiment, the after-hours plunge Thursday brought Intuitive Machines stock back to levels not seen since October 2024.

Investors need to brace for volatility when investing in space.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Stocks Rebound as Powell Reassures Markets that the Economy is Fine - US Market Wrap
data.description:
A roller-coaster week for markets concluded on the same note, with stocks whipsawing as traders attempted to make sense of a slew of headlines about the economy, tariffs, and geopolitical developments.

Just minutes after the S&P 500 fell more than 1%, the gauge staged a “oversold bounce” as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell stated that the economy is fine. The Nasdaq 100 briefly crossed the threshold for a correction. Bonds fell. In the first indication of a positive response from President Vladimir Putin to US counterpart Donald Trump’s call for a ceasefire, Russia was said to be willing to discuss a temporary truce in Ukraine. The dollar experienced its worst week since November 2022.

Markets have had a whirlwind week as tariffs have reached a fever pitch, sending the S&P 500 into a tailspin and briefly driving it below a closely watched technical level: its 200-day moving average. Despite a late-week rebound, the gauge closed with its worst weekly selloff since September.

Wall Street also kept an eye on the latest economic reports. Last month, US job growth remained stable while the unemployment rate rose, presenting a mixed picture of a market teetering on the edge of rapidly changing government policy. Nonfarm payrolls increased by 151,000 in February, following a downward revision to the previous month. The unemployment rate increased to 4.1%.

data.title: Ibovespa Advances for 3rd Session
data.description:
The Ibovespa reversed early losses to close 1.4% higher at 125,035 on Friday, extending gains for the third straight session as investors absorbed disappointing Q4 economic data and measures to curb food inflation.

A surprising 1.0% drop in household consumption and a mere 0.1% growth in services raised concerns over an economic slowdown, with GDP expanding just 0.2%—less than half of market expectations—reinforcing views that Brazil’s strong growth phase has ended and prompting speculation of a more cautious central bank.

Meanwhile, the government's decision to cut import tariffs on key food commodities has dampened bets on further aggressive tightening.

Major banks and meat processors led the advance, with Banco Santander and JBS rising 3.2% and 2.8%, respectively, while mega caps Petrobras, Vale, Ambev, and WEG gained between 1.2% and 1.7%.

The São Paulo exchange posted a robust 1.8% weekly advance.

News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Friday at 4 PM ET
data.description:
Global Insurer Allianz in Talks for $3 Billion-Plus Viridium Deal

Germany's Allianz is in exclusive talks to acquire Viridium, a consolidator of closed books of insurance policies, in a deal that would bolster its domestic operations.

Tariff Fights Keep the Fed in a Holding Pattern on Rates

Fed Chair Jerome Powell says central bank positioned "to wait for greater clarity" on economic outlook.

You Can't Escape Politics. Your Investing Decisions Can.

Some ETFs match your portfolio to your ideology. There's just one problem: The stock market doesn't care how you vote.

U.K. Pension Manager NEST Plots Private-Markets Investing Spree

The fast-growing defined-contribution plan aims to pump billions into alternative assets while avoiding heavy fees.

Blackstone Raises Largest Commercial Property Debt Fund With $8 Billion Haul

The fund took two years to build and will be active in North America, Europe and Australia.

Private Equity Wants a Piece of Your 401(k)

Companies like Apollo and KKR are angling for retirement savers with new funds. Investors in 401(k)s should be wary.

Blackstone vs. BlackRock: The Greatest Wall Street Frenemy Story Ever Told

Stephen Schwarzman and Larry Fink are increasingly all up in each other's business. Just look at BlackRock's new Panama Canal deal.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Just Group, Malaysian banks and more in the latest Market Talks covering financial services.

Trump Signs Executive Order Officially Establishing Crypto Reserve

The U.S.'s stockpile will favor bitcoin and won't include purchases that cost taxpayers money, the Trump administration says.

The Polarizing Crypto Executive Who Wooed His Way Into Trump's Orbit

Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse stands to benefit from the inclusion of the XRP token in a U.S. digital-asset stockpile.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Friday at 4 PM ET
data.description:
Crude prices end higher on report of U.S. plan to refill its oil reserve

Oil futures settled higher Friday, buoyed by reported U.S. plans to refill the nation's Strategic Petroleum Reserve to full capacity, which would lead to tighter global supplies.

Nearly Half of Energy Executives Think We Won't Reach Net Zero Until 2070

Rising capital project costs and a lack of optimism around ESG are pushing back environmental goals, according to a survey of industry leaders from across oil and gas, utilities, chemicals, mining, and agribusiness.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures and more in the latest Market Talks covering energy and utilities

Harbour Energy Shares Sink to Record Low After Profit Hit by U.K. Windfall Tax

The oil-and-gas producer swung to a loss in 2024 as the energy profits levy continued to drag on results and its CEO described the U.K. as a difficult fiscal and regulatory environment in which to operate.

Sunnova Bondholders Engage Advisers Following KKR Deal, Going-Concern Warning

A group of bondholders in Sunnova Energy International has organized and hired legal counsel after the rooftop solar panel company issued a going concern notice and said it raised financing from KKR.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories rose by 3.6 million barrels last week, which was more than expected, resuming an upward trend after a surprise drawdown the week before.

DTEK in Talks With U.S. Suppliers to Boost Gas Supply to Europe

Ukraine's largest private energy company is in discussions with U.S. suppliers to bring more natural gas to Europe as part of a broader expansion, its CEO said in an interview.

Nuclear Power's Revival Is Here. What Do You Do With All the Radioactive Waste?

Tons of radioactive refuse go into temporary storage every year, and new efforts to store nuclear waste away from reactor sites are getting pushback.

Oil Outlook Clouded by OPEC+ Output, Trade Tariff Risks

Higher-than-expected supply and a potential demand hit due to softer U.S. activity and escalating tariffs pose downside risks to its oil price forecast, said Goldman Sachs.

U.S. Refiners Import Lots of Oil. Trump's Tariffs Spell Trouble.

Supplies from Canada and Mexico are at the heart of the U.S. oil industry.

data.title: NI Holdings 4Q EPS 47c >NODK
Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1546 ET - CME livestock futures settled higher, with live cattle futures finishing up 2% to $2.00275 a pound while lean hogs close up 0.7% to 87.7 cents a pound. For cattle, it's the first session where cattle finished above the $2 a pound mark in just over a month. For both, delays in tariffs on Mexico and Canada were supportive for futures. "A lot of tariff influence on livestock today," says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting. "Hogs got a bump from hopes we see little disruption to exports." Hog and pork exports are a big source of demand for U.S. pork, with Mexico being the largest buyer. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1522 ET - Oil futures post losses for the week in which markets were hit by OPEC+ saying its output increases will go ahead in April, uncertainty over U.S. tariffs, and a bearish build in U.S. crude stocks. Despite today's 1% gain, WTI stretches its losing streak to seven weeks, settling at $67.04 a barrel. WTI "may be finding some support," says Alex Hodes of StoneX in a note. "Although the highs of the year are likely in the rearview, there appears to be more price risk to the upside than downside at current price levels." He sees bullish risk with reports of OPEC+ rethinking the announced production returns and potential disruption to Iranian supplies. Brent rises 1.3% to $70.36 a barrel, and is down 3.4% on the week. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1517 ET - U.S. natural gas futures settle higher as the market looks beyond bearish near-term fundamentals including milder March weather than previously forecast, higher production and lower heating and LNG demand. While this week's inventory report showed a below-estimate 80 Bcf withdrawal from storage, stocks remain in deficit against a year ago and the five-year average, raising expectations of strong refill demand over the summer. "The market is staring at an uphill battle to bring stocks back to healthy levels heading into next winter," Andy Huenefeld of Pinebrook Energy Advisors said in a note. The Nymex front month settles up 2.3% at $4.399/mmBtu, up 15% from a week ago. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1431 ET - With tariffs delayed on a range of goods from Mexico and Canada, the attention of grain traders pivots back toward weather, especially in Brazil. Rainfall there is good for some drier crops while others already have enough water. "Corn is higher on concerns over the safrinha crop in Brazil and firming basis values there," says Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting. He adds that basis for soybeans in Brazil is also firming, which in turn provided support for both futures contracts on the CBOT. Next week's WASDE is expected to show smaller corn inventories globally. Most-active corn is up 1.1%, while soybeans are down 0.2% and wheat is off 0.3%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1337 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. was unchanged this week at 486, or 18 fewer than a year ago, oil service company Baker Hughes reports. Natural gas rigs slipped by 1 to 101, and were down 14 on the year. In a report this week, the EIA noted a 32% drop in natural gas rigs over the past two years, led by declines in the Appalachia and Haynesville regions, coinciding with low natural gas prices and adoption of advanced drilling and completion technologies. Producers responded to low prices by drilling less and curtailing production which increased inventories of drilled but uncompleted wells. "If natural gas demand and prices continue to rise, producers could be in a better economic position to complete these wells, potentially allowing them to quickly increase production," the EIA said. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1316 ET - CBOT grain futures are mixed in midday trade, with corn higher while soybeans and wheat fall back. Traders are attempting to balance what risk is looking like for grains and commodities in general heading into the weekend. "Massive headline risk lingers in the background, but trade policy aside it's just premature to be overly bearish at current prices until there's guidance in the North Hemisphere summer climate pattern," says Daniel Flynn of Price Futures Group in a note. Trading earlier today was dominated by markets attempting to fully factor in partial tariff delays granted for Mexico and Canada. Most-active corn is up 0.9%, soybeans fall 0.5%, and wheat drop 0.6%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1220 ET - The February jobs report supports the view that the U.S. labor market is roughly balanced, Federal Reserve governor Adriana Kugler says. Speaking at a conference in Portugal Friday evening, Kugler notes that at 4.1%, the unemployment rate has stayed in a narrow range since last summer, and that the 151,000 new jobs created in February was not so far from the monthly average of 177,000 over the past six months. Average hourly earnings have climbed by about 4% over the past 12 months: "With strong productivity growth in recent quarters, I do not believe wages are a significant source of inflation pressure," Kugler says. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1220 ET - Inflation pressures remain in the economy, and as a result it might be appropriate for the Fed to stay on pause for a while longer, Federal Reserve governor Adriana Kugler says. Speaking at a conference in Portugal, Kugler notes that U.S. inflation expectations have climbed recently, and also observes "considerable uncertainty over the inflationary effects of new policies and policy proposals." "Given the recent increase in inflation expectations and the key inflation categories that have not shown progress toward our 2 percent target, it could be appropriate to continue holding the policy rate at its current level for some time," Kugler says. (matt.grossman@wsj.com, @mattgrossman)

1215 ET - Base metal prices fall but are set to finish the week higher, with LME three-month copper down 0.9% at $9,601.50 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.1% at $2,700 a ton. President Trump's tariff threats have driven New York-traded copper prices up significantly compared to LME prices, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen says in a note. Earlier this week, Trump repeated his intention to tariff copper, driving prices of U.S. futures sharply higher. The copper market may have reacted prematurely, as the investigation into the metal under Section 232 of the Trade Act normally takes months--but the 25% tariff threat was clearly higher than the market expected, and U.S. traders are now scrambling to price in the correct level, Hansen says. The U.S. copper market will likely exhibit greater volatility, he adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1159 ET - CME live cattle futures return to climbing after a quick breather through yesterday's trading session. Most-active futures are up 1.7% in morning trade, with the delays made to the implementation of tariffs of Mexico and Canada seen as a positive for U.S. beef, as the U.S. is currently a net importer of cattle — getting most of their imports from Mexico. "Ag market price action on Thursday was primarily a function of the Trump Administration reversion to the USMCA... this supported ag markets--including cattle and feeder cattle," says AgResource in a note. Lean hogs are up 0.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1117 ET - News of the partial tariff pullback on Mexico and Canada is a relief for farmers heading into spring planting, as tariffs on key fertilizer ingredient potash are also being negotiated lower. Canada is the world's largest producer and exporter of potash. "The decisions, which are in effect until April 2, are welcome news for American farmers," says Naomi Blohm of Total Farm Marketing in a note. Most-active corn rises 0.5%, soybeans inch up 0.1%, and wheat slides 0.8%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1018 ET - Foreign-exchange traders have yet to come to terms that the heightened uncertainty over the Trump administration's tariffs "is still really, really bad for Canada," says Brent Donnelly, head of forex-research firm Spectra Markets. He says upcoming Canadian economic indicators are going to take on paramount importance, as they will provide evidence about how tariff uncertainty is weighing on activity. Some analysts say the lackluster Canada jobs report is a sign of a pullback in hiring. Donnelly says a downturn in Canadian data "will lead to massive CAD selling," adding "the market loves the theme but hasn't been able to hold on because of the unexpected reaction in USDCAD." CAD largely held steady this week relative to USD. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Hess Corp Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >HES
data.description:
Hess Corp. (HES) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 07, 2025.

Annual Cash Incentive Plan

On March 4, 2025, the Compensation and Management Development Committee (the "Committee") of the Board of Directors ("Board") of Hess Corporation (the "Company") approved annual incentive targets under the Company's Annual Incentive Plan (the "Plan") for all of the Company's full-time employees worldwide, including the Company's chief executive officer, chief financial officer and three other most highly compensated executive officers (the "Named Executive Officers"). The Plan is intended to promote alignment of pay and performance and an enhanced focus on creating long-term stockholder value.

Payout on awards is determined based on attainment of pre-established enterprise level metrics and individual performance objectives. The following are the enterprise metrics, each with pre-established threshold, target and maximum performance goals:

*

Environment, health and safety (5 measures):

*

Bakken routine flare rate

*

Critical inspection compliance

*

Severe + significant safety incident rate

*

Loss of primary containment rate

*

Safety observations

*

Controllable Production;

*

Exploration and production capital and exploratory spend; and

*

Controllable operated cash costs.

As part of its ongoing assessment of the Company's compensation programs, the Committee determined to continue to use the enterprise-wide performance modifier to increase or decrease payout of the Plan by up to 25% based on five evaluation themes: strategy execution; capital allocation and risk management; social responsibility and stakeholder engagement; culture, people and leadership; and environment and sustainability.

The Committee also establishes annual incentive targets for each Named Executive Officer based upon position, responsibilities and competitive practice.

Payouts for the Named Executive Officers range from 0% to 200% of target based on attainment of the pre-established enterprise metrics, the strategic performance modifier and individual performance compared with individual goals pre-established at the beginning of the fiscal year.

Long-Term Incentive Program

On March 4, 2025, the Committee approved the value of awards to the Named Executive Officers under the Company's long-term incentive program for 2025 (the "2025 Program"), effective March 6, 2025.

Due to the Company's pending with merger with Chevron Corporation, the Named Executive Officers, including Mr. Hess, received long-term incentive awards in the form of restricted stock that vest in equal installments over a three-year period beginning on the first anniversary of the grant date, subject to accelerated vesting on certain terminations of employment pursuant to the terms of the Company's 2017 Long-Term Incentive Plan, a copy of which was filed as Exhibit 10(1) to the Company's Form 8-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on June 13, 2017, and the form of restricted stock award agreement, a copy of which was filed as Exhibit 10(1) to the Company's Form 8-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 8, 2024.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/4447/000119312525049890/d909883d8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/4447/000119312525049890/0001193125-25-049890-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC) SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC), a Delaware Statutory Trust formed to hold Zcash (ZEC), has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The Trust, which provides investors with exposure to ZEC through securities, has reported significant financial growth, strategic initiatives, and outlined the challenges and risks it faces in the evolving digital asset market.

Financial Highlights

  • Net realized and unrealized gain (loss) on investment: $11,248 thousand, driven by ZEC price appreciation from $27.69 per ZEC as of December 31, 2023, to $56.12 per ZEC as of December 31, 2024.
  • Net increase (decrease) in net assets resulting from operations: $10,952 thousand, which consisted of the net realized and unrealized gain on investment in ZEC, less the Sponsor’s Fee of $296 thousand.
  • Net assets: $22,040 thousand as of December 31, 2024, a 146% increase for the year, resulting from ZEC price appreciation and the contribution of approximately 78,192 ZEC with a value of $2,127 thousand to the Trust in connection with Share creations during the period.

Business Highlights

  • Trust Overview: Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC) is designed to hold Zcash (ZEC), a digital asset similar to Bitcoin but with privacy-preserving features. The Trust aims to provide investors with exposure to ZEC through securities.
  • Zcash Network Features: Zcash offers enhanced privacy features using zk-SNARKs, allowing for shielded transactions that protect transaction details. Approximately 45% of transactions are unshielded, 45% involve one shielded party, and 10% are fully shielded.
  • Mining and Supply: Zcash uses the Equihash algorithm, which does not require ASICs, leading to less centralized mining. Zcash has a maximum supply of 21 million coins, with a current circulating supply of 16.3 million as of December 31, 2024.
  • Market Position: As of December 31, 2024, Zcash was the 104th largest digital asset by market capitalization. The Trust holds approximately 2.4% of the circulating ZEC.
  • Trading and Liquidity: The Trust's shares are quoted on OTCQX under the ticker symbol 'ZCSH'. The shares have traded at both premiums and discounts to the Trust's NAV, with a maximum premium of 240% and a maximum discount of 55% from October 18, 2021, to December 31, 2024.
  • Custody and Security: The Trust's ZEC is held in cold storage by Coinbase Custody Trust Company, LLC, ensuring enhanced security through geographically distributed private key shards.
  • Operational Activities: The Trust issues shares in exchange for ZEC and may sell ZEC to cover expenses. It does not currently operate a redemption program, limiting arbitrage opportunities.
  • Regulatory Environment: The Trust operates under a complex regulatory landscape, with ongoing scrutiny from U.S. and international regulators regarding digital assets. The Trust may face challenges if ZEC is classified as a security.
  • Future Outlook: The Trust aims to reflect the value of ZEC held, less expenses, but acknowledges challenges in meeting this objective due to market conditions and regulatory factors.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Corporate Reorganization: The Trust underwent a corporate reorganization on January 1, 2025, where Grayscale Investments, LLC merged with Grayscale Operating, LLC, resulting in Grayscale Operating, LLC succeeding to all rights and obligations. This reorganization is not expected to materially impact the Trust's operations.
  • Capital Management: The Trust does not maintain a cash balance and relies on selling ZEC to cover expenses, specifically the Sponsor's Fee. The Trust's shares are quoted on OTCQX, and the Trust has engaged in periodic share creations, receiving ZEC in exchange. The Trust has not accepted redemption requests from shareholders and has no current plans to initiate a redemption program. DCG, the indirect parent company of the Sponsor, has an authorization to purchase up to $10 million worth of Shares, with $2 million purchased by June 2022, but no further purchases through March 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The Trust may consider operating a redemption program in the future, subject to regulatory approval and the Sponsor's discretion. The Trust will continue to monitor its principal market annually and conduct quarterly analyses to ensure optimal market conditions for ZEC trading. The reorganization involving Grayscale Investments, LLC is expected to streamline operations without impacting the Trust's strategic direction.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Volatility: The company faces significant risks related to the extreme volatility of digital asset trading prices, particularly ZEC, which could materially affect the value of the Shares. The digital asset market is highly speculative and subject to rapid changes, which may result in substantial losses for investors.
  • Regulatory Risks: The digital asset market is largely unregulated, leading to potential risks of fraud, market manipulation, and operational failures on Digital Asset Trading Platforms. This lack of regulation and transparency could negatively affect the value of digital assets and the Shares. Additionally, the Trust's reliance on third-party service providers poses operational risks, as any disruptions or replacements could challenge the safekeeping of the Trust's ZEC and its operations.
  • Regulatory Environment: Regulatory risks are significant, with potential changes in U.S. and foreign regulations that could restrict the use of ZEC, mining activities, or the operation of digital asset markets. The classification of ZEC as a security could also lead to extraordinary expenses or termination of the Trust. Recent regulatory actions against major digital asset platforms have increased scrutiny and could lead to further market volatility.
  • Operational Risks: Management has noted the impact of recent developments in the digital asset economy, which have led to extreme volatility and disruption in digital asset markets. This includes the insolvency of major industry participants and increased regulatory scrutiny, which have contributed to a loss of confidence and liquidity in the market. The Trust does not actively manage its holdings to mitigate these risks, which could result in significant value loss for the Shares.
  • Market Risks: The Trust is exposed to market risks due to the volatility of ZEC prices, which are influenced by a variety of factors including global supply, trading activity, and regulatory changes. The lack of an arbitrage mechanism due to the holding period under Rule 144 and the absence of an ongoing redemption program means that the Shares may trade at a substantial premium or discount to the NAV per Share. This could result in losses for shareholders if the Shares trade significantly above or below the Trust’s NAV per Share.

SEC Filing: Grayscale Zcash Trust (ZEC) [ ZCSH ] - 10-K - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Grayscale Ethereum Classic Trust (ETC) SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Grayscale Ethereum Classic Trust (ETC), a Delaware Statutory Trust formed to hold Ethereum Classic (ETC), has released its 2024 10-K report. The Trust aims to provide investors with exposure to ETC through shares that represent ownership in the Trust. The report details the Trust's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the volatile digital asset market.

Financial Highlights

  • Net realized and unrealized gain (loss) on investment: $36,254 thousand, driven by ETC price appreciation from $21.99 per ETC as of December 31, 2023, to $25.13 per ETC as of December 31, 2024.
  • Net increase (decrease) in net assets resulting from operations: $29,097 thousand, which consisted of the net realized and unrealized gain on investment in ETC, less the Sponsor’s Fee of $7,157.
  • Net assets: $283,055 thousand as of December 31, 2024, an 11% increase for the year, resulting from ETC price appreciation, partially offset by the withdrawal of approximately 285,150 ETC to pay the Sponsor’s Fee.

Business Highlights

  • Trust Overview: Grayscale Ethereum Classic Trust (ETC) is designed to hold Ethereum Classic (ETC) and provide investors with exposure to ETC through shares that represent ownership in the Trust.
  • ETC Network and Operations: Ethereum Classic operates on a decentralized network, allowing for peer-to-peer transactions and smart contract execution. The network maintains a proof-of-work consensus mechanism, differentiating it from Ethereum, which has transitioned to proof-of-stake.
  • Market Position: As of December 31, 2024, Ethereum Classic was the 35th largest digital asset by market capitalization, with a 24-hour trading volume of approximately $76.7 million and an aggregate market value of $3.8 billion.
  • Trust Holdings: The Trust holds approximately 8% of the ETC in circulation, but this position does not enable the Trust to influence the development of the Ethereum Classic Network.
  • Share Trading and Premiums/Discounts: Shares of the Trust are quoted on OTCQX under the ticker symbol 'ETCG'. The shares have historically traded at both premiums and discounts to the Trust's net asset value (NAV), with a maximum premium of 458% and a maximum discount of 77% observed between May 2018 and December 2024.
  • Custody and Security: The Trust's ETC is secured by Coinbase Custody Trust Company, LLC, using cold storage mechanisms to protect private key shards. This enhances security and minimizes credit risk compared to other financial products.
  • Incidental Rights and IR Virtual Currency: The Trust may acquire Incidental Rights and IR Virtual Currency through forks or airdrops. However, these are generally abandoned unless they can be safely and practically managed.
  • ETC Creation and Supply: ETC is created through a mining process with a current block reward of 2.6 ETC. The total supply is capped at 210 million ETC, with approximately 150 million ETC in circulation as of December 31, 2024.
  • Network Upgrades: The Ethereum Classic Network undergoes periodic upgrades to maintain interoperability with Ethereum and enhance network capabilities. Recent upgrades include the Phoenix hardfork and the Thanos upgrade.
  • Regulatory Environment: The digital asset industry faces increasing regulatory scrutiny, particularly following the collapse of FTX in 2022. The SEC has taken actions against major trading platforms, impacting the digital asset market.
  • Future Outlook: The Trust aims to provide a cost-effective and convenient way for investors to gain exposure to ETC. However, the lack of a redemption program and regulatory challenges may continue to affect the trading price of the shares relative to the Trust's NAV.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Corporate Reorganization: The Trust underwent a significant internal corporate reorganization on January 1, 2025, where Grayscale Investments, LLC merged with Grayscale Operating, LLC, resulting in Grayscale Operating, LLC succeeding to all rights and obligations of the former entity. This reorganization is not expected to materially impact the Trust's operations.
  • Redemption Program: The Trust has not accepted redemption requests from shareholders and has no current plans to seek regulatory approval for a redemption program, indicating a strategic focus on maintaining its current operational structure.
  • Capital Management: The Trust has not maintained a cash balance since inception, relying on the sale of ETC to cover expenses, specifically the Sponsor's Fee. The Trust's shares have been quoted on OTCQX, and the price has varied significantly from the Trust's NAV per Share, often trading at a discount. The Trust's parent company, DCG, has an authorization to purchase up to $200 million worth of shares across various Grayscale investment products, although no purchases were made under this authorization from January 1, 2022, through March 3, 2025. The Trust has distributed 13,993,800 shares to accredited investors under Rule 506(c) of Regulation D, receiving an aggregate of 13,235,272.04722510 ETC in exchange.
  • Future Outlook: The Trust's future outlook includes the continuation of its current operational model without a redemption program, unless regulatory approval is obtained. The reorganization involving Grayscale Operating, LLC and Grayscale Investments Sponsors, LLC is expected to streamline operations without altering the rights or obligations under existing contracts. The Trust will continue to focus on maintaining its ETC holdings and managing expenses through the sale of ETC, with no immediate plans for changes in its capital management strategy.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company faces significant market risks due to the extreme volatility of digital asset prices, including Ethereum Classic (ETC), which could materially affect the value of the Shares. The digital asset market is subject to rapid changes and lacks standardized regulation, leading to potential market manipulation and fraud. The emergence of new digital assets and technologies could also negatively impact the demand for ETC.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include the reliance on third-party service providers for essential functions. Any disruption or replacement of these providers could pose challenges to the safekeeping of the Trust’s ETC and the overall operations of the Trust. Additionally, the Trust's lack of an ongoing redemption program and the ability to halt creations may result in the Shares trading at a substantial premium or discount to the NAV per Share.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks are heightened by potential changes in U.S. and foreign regulations that could restrict the use of ETC or affect the operation of digital asset markets. The classification of ETC as a security could lead to significant expenses or termination of the Trust. Recent regulatory actions against major digital asset trading platforms have increased scrutiny and could lead to further market instability.
  • Emerging Risks: New and emerging risks include the potential for forks or clones of the Ethereum Classic Network, which could adversely affect the value of the Shares. The digital asset market's reliance on stablecoins, which are subject to their own regulatory and operational risks, could also impact the market for ETC.
  • Management's Discussion and Analysis: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the volatile digital asset market and the potential impact of regulatory changes. The Trust does not actively manage its holdings to mitigate volatility, which could lead to significant fluctuations in the value of the Shares. Management is focused on monitoring regulatory developments and maintaining relationships with service providers to ensure operational stability.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: The Trust is exposed to market risks related to the price volatility of ETC and the broader digital asset market. The lack of regulation and transparency in digital asset trading platforms increases the risk of fraud and manipulation, which could adversely affect the value of the Shares. The Trust's performance is also subject to risks associated with the liquidity of digital asset markets and the potential for significant price fluctuations.

SEC Filing: Grayscale Ethereum Classic Trust (ETC) [ ETCG ] - 10-K - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Boxlight Corp expected to post a loss of $2.82 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Boxlight Corp is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on March 11 (estimated) for the period ending December 31 2024

  • The Duluth Georgia-based company is expected to report a 22.5% decrease in revenue to $30.08 million from $38.81 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Boxlight Corp is for a loss of $2.82 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Boxlight Corp is $6.25​, above​ its last closing price of $1.72. ​​​

data.title: Castellum Insider Sold Shares Worth $717,450, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Jay O Wright, 10% Owner, Director, General Counsel, Secretary, and Executive Vice President, Strategy, on March 05, 2025, sold 500,000 shares in Castellum for $717,450. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Wright has control over a total of 9,523,673 common shares of the company, with 9,523,673 shares held directly.
